prompt: isometric pixel-art game tiles for a top-down RPG map. Each tile is a DIFFERENT large object standing FREE on a small flat patch of packed brown dirt — the object itself rises straight up and IS the sprite. Do NOT put it on a raised block, cube, column, box, crate or pedestal, and do NOT draw the ground as a tall raised cube: just a small low patch at the base, everything else transparent. Go for maximum variety — different shapes, species, ages and conditions, no two tiles alike. The set: 1) a tall stack of balanced boulders 2) a ruined mud-brick tower 3) a leaning telegraph pole 4) a tall totem of stacked bones 5) a tall dry cypress 6) a wind-carved dirt hoodoo 7) a crumbling adobe chimney 8) a dirt cliff face with dangling roots 9) a dead snag draped with dry vines 10) a tall termite spire. cozy hand-crafted top-down RPG pixel art, rich natural texture, cohesive harmonious palette, soft directional shading, high quality game asset, transparent background
